why: Canonical seed-taxonomy tag (namespace.md:320). Round-trips under the single-segment capability-tag grammar (CAPABILITY_TAG_RE, patterns.ts:21).
Plain lowercase tag, no separators — the simplest valid form.
Two chars is the length floor. Guards the off-by-one at the mandatory trailing [a-z0-9] in CAPABILITY_TAG_RE.
patterns.ts:21 explicitly excludes single-char tags ('none in the seed taxonomy and they collide with 1-char structured-id forms').
The tag alphabet is lowercase-only; uppercase would break case-sensitive NATS-subject and KV-key embedding.
The '--' rejection mirrors DID_RE's negative lookahead (RFC-0001). Keeps tags safe to embed in subjects, KV keys, and file paths.
The mandatory final [a-z0-9] forbids a trailing hyphen — a divergence from stack-slug (RFC-0001), which permits one.
First char MUST be a letter — a segment that could appear in a NATS subject must not start with a digit.

THE C-3 INCOMPATIBILITY. cortex CAPABILITY_ID_REGEX (capability.ts:172) ACCEPTS '_'; the myelin capability-tag REJECTS it. A cortex agent advertising an underscore id is unmatchable by an envelope requirements[] tag. Blocked on OPEN DECISION §6.1.

THE C-3 INCOMPATIBILITY. '.' is a NATS subject separator; the cortex dotted-compound grammar accepts 'code.review' as TWO segments, the myelin single-segment tag rejects it. Blocked on OPEN DECISION §6.1.

The capability-tag GRAMMAR accepts 'dead-letter'. namespace.md:172-174 (RFC-0002) declares it a RESERVED segment that MUST be a publish-time validation error, but no code enforces the reservation (audit tasks/dead-letter-reservation-unenforced). This vector documents that the grammar layer is not the enforcement layer — a Security finding, §9.4.

THE MASKING CASE. 'code-review' is accepted by BOTH the myelin single-segment capability-tag AND the cortex dotted-compound capability-id, so an interop test drawn only from the shared seed taxonomy PASSES and HIDES the C-3 incompatibility. An adversarial suite MUST also include an id valid in exactly one grammar (see underscore/dot/compound vectors).
Live cortex production id (audit discovery-econ). VALID under CAPABILITY_ID_REGEX, INVALID as a myelin capability-tag (contains '.'). The C-3 incompatibility made concrete.
Live cortex production id carrying BOTH a '.' and an '_' — doubly unexpressible as a capability-tag. Two independent reasons this cannot cross to the myelin requirements[] side.

cortex sub-capability compound. SEMANTIC HAZARD: a myelin task requiring the tag 'code-review' does NOT match an agent advertising 'code-review.typescript' under the shipped exact-tag match (docs/discovery.md: caps.includes(tag)), though cortex prefix-matching would. Match semantics diverge, not just grammar.
Anti-spoof fast-reject (verify.ts:56-60): the signing stamp DID must equal the advertisement actor-DID. An agent cannot advertise another agent's capabilities.

R2 transition boundary (advertisement-identity.ts). An advertisement carrying BOTH the deprecated 'principal' and canonical 'identity' actor-DID keys is refused BEFORE canonicalization — a differing pair is an attack, an identical pair a producer bug; either way not silently coalesced.
register.ts:58 requires maxConcurrent to be a positive integer. A zero/negative/non-integer ceiling would either exclude the agent from all dispatch or overflow the executor pool.

load is clamped to [0,1] at signing time (register.ts clampLoad). NOTE: load is NOT re-validated at verify time (verify.ts) — a hand-forged registration signed off-path could carry an out-of-range load. Documented in §9.1.

SECURITY GAP (§9.1). Neither signCapabilityRegistration nor verifyCapabilityRegistration checks capabilities[] against ANY grammar — an advertisement with ungrammatical, reserved, or wildcard-adjacent tags VERIFIES today. This vector pins CURRENT wire behaviour; it flips to ok:false once OPEN DECISION §6.4 mandates tag validation at the trust boundary (change-log event, not a silent edit).
